<content>
1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Code Graph Phase Runner
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: // Typed phase-DAG orchestrator for the structural indexing flow.
5: // Each phase declares its name, its dependency outputs (`inputs`),
6: // and the output key it produces (`output`). The runner validates
7: // the DAG (duplicate names, missing deps, cycles), topologically
8: // sorts the nodes, and invokes each phase with a typed map that
9: // contains ONLY the outputs of phases listed in its `inputs` —
10: // undeclared upstream outputs are not visible to the phase body.
11: //
12: // Owner-local clean-room implementation. The DAG shape (declared
13: // inputs/outputs, duplicate/missing/cycle rejection, dependency-only output
14: // passing) is taken from the phase-runner pattern; no upstream source/schema
15: // text is reused.
16: 
17: /**
18:  * A single phase in the indexing DAG.
19:  *
20:  * - `name`     — unique within a runner invocation (duplicates rejected)
21:  * - `inputs`   — names of phases this phase depends on; the runner
22:  *                passes ONLY these outputs in `deps`. Listing a name
23:  *                that does not exist in the runner is rejected.
24:  * - `output`   — the key this phase publishes (defaults to `name`).
25:  *                Downstream phases reference it via `inputs`.
26:  * - `run(deps)` — receives a map keyed by `inputs[i]` (each value
27:  *                produced by that named phase). Returns the phase's
28:  *                output value (typed `O`).
29:  */
30: export interface Phase<I extends Record<string, unknown> = Record<string, unknown>, O = unknown> {
31:   readonly name: string;
32:   readonly inputs: readonly string[];
33:   readonly output?: string;
34:   run(deps: I): Promise<O> | O;
35: }
36: 
37: /** Error thrown when a runner invocation cannot construct a valid DAG. */
38: export class PhaseRunnerError extends Error {
39:   readonly kind:
40:     | 'duplicate-phase'
41:     | 'duplicate-output'
42:     | 'missing-dependency'
43:     | 'cycle-detected'
44:     | 'phase-failure'
45:     | 'invalid-key';
46:   readonly phaseName?: string;
47: 
48:   constructor(
49:     kind:
50:       | 'duplicate-phase'
51:       | 'duplicate-output'
52:       | 'missing-dependency'
53:       | 'cycle-detected'
54:       | 'phase-failure'
55:       | 'invalid-key',
56:     message: string,
57:     phaseName?: string,
58:     options?: { cause?: unknown },
59:   ) {
60:     super(message, options);
61:     this.name = 'PhaseRunnerError';
62:     this.kind = kind;
63:     this.phaseName = phaseName;
64:   }
65: }
66: 
67: // Runtime guards for phase keys. TypeScript enforces these at compile time,
68: // but consumers of the exported API may pass values
69: // through type-erased boundaries (JSON, dynamic loading, JS callers).
70: // Reject empty strings, non-strings, and strings containing control
71: // characters BEFORE DAG construction so the failure path is predictable.
72: const PHASE_KEY_BLOCKED = /[\x00-\x1F\x7F]/;
73: const PHASE_KEY_MAX_LENGTH = 256;
74: 
75: function validatePhaseKey(value: unknown, role: string, phaseName?: string): string {
76:   if (typeof value !== 'string') {
77:     throw new PhaseRunnerError(
78:       'invalid-key',
79:       `Phase runner rejected: ${role} must be a string, got ${value === null ? 'null' : typeof value}`,
80:       phaseName,
81:     );
82:   }
83:   if (value.length === 0) {
84:     throw new PhaseRunnerError(
85:       'invalid-key',
86:       `Phase runner rejected: ${role} must be a non-empty string`,
87:       phaseName,
88:     );
89:   }
90:   if (value.length > PHASE_KEY_MAX_LENGTH) {
91:     throw new PhaseRunnerError(
92:       'invalid-key',
93:       `Phase runner rejected: ${role} exceeds ${PHASE_KEY_MAX_LENGTH}-character cap`,
94:       phaseName,
95:     );
96:   }
97:   if (PHASE_KEY_BLOCKED.test(value)) {
98:     throw new PhaseRunnerError(
99:       'invalid-key',
100:       `Phase runner rejected: ${role} contains control characters`,
101:       phaseName,
102:     );
103:   }
104:   return value;
105: }
106: 
107: /** Map of phase output keys to the value produced by that phase. */
108: export type PhaseOutputs = Record<string, unknown>;
109: 
110: function outputKey(phase: Phase): string {
111:   return phase.output ?? phase.name;
112: }
113: 
114: /**
115:  * Validate the DAG and return a topological order of phase names.
116:  *
117:  * Rejection paths (in evaluation order):
118:  *   1. Duplicate phase name        → PhaseRunnerError('duplicate-phase')
119:  *   2. Duplicate `output` key      → PhaseRunnerError('duplicate-output')
120:  *      across phases
121:  *   3. Dependency that does not    → PhaseRunnerError('missing-dependency')
122:  *      resolve to any phase output
123:  *   4. Any cycle                    → PhaseRunnerError('cycle-detected')
124:  */
125: export function topologicalSort(phases: readonly Phase[]): string[] {
126:   // Validate every phase's runtime keys BEFORE DAG construction. TypeScript
127:   // erases at runtime, so callers loading phases through
128:   // JSON / dynamic imports / JS bridges may bypass the compile-time
129:   // contract and reach this function with malformed shapes.
130:   for (const phase of phases) {
131:     validatePhaseKey(phase.name, 'phase.name');
132:     if (phase.output !== undefined) {
133:       validatePhaseKey(phase.output, 'phase.output', phase.name);
134:     }
135:     for (const input of phase.inputs ?? []) {
136:       validatePhaseKey(input, 'phase.inputs entry', phase.name);
137:     }
138:   }
139: 
140:   const phasesByName = new Map<string, Phase>();
141:   for (const phase of phases) {
142:     if (phasesByName.has(phase.name)) {
143:       throw new PhaseRunnerError(
144:         'duplicate-phase',
145:         `Phase runner rejected: duplicate phase name "${phase.name}"`,
146:         phase.name,
147:       );
148:     }
149:     phasesByName.set(phase.name, phase);
150:   }
151: 
152:   // Build output-key → phase-name lookup AND reject any duplicate output
153:   // keys. Two phases publishing the same output
154:   // key are just as ambiguous as two phases sharing a name —
155:   // dependents that reference that key cannot deterministically
156:   // resolve which producer's value they receive. Mirror the
157:   // duplicate-name rejection so the failure modes stay symmetric.
158:   const phaseNamesByOutputKey = new Map<string, string>();
159:   for (const phase of phases) {
160:     const key = outputKey(phase);
161:     const existingProducer = phaseNamesByOutputKey.get(key);
162:     if (existingProducer !== undefined && existingProducer !== phase.name) {
163:       throw new PhaseRunnerError(
164:         'duplicate-output',
165:         `Phase runner rejected: phases "${existingProducer}" and "${phase.name}" both publish output key "${key}"`,
166:         phase.name,
167:       );
168:     }
169:     phaseNamesByOutputKey.set(key, phase.name);
170:   }
171: 
172:   // Also reject when a phase's `output` key collides with a
173:   // *different* phase's `name` (which would also cause ambiguous
174:   // dependency resolution). This is the second half of the
175:   // symmetry: a phase named `X` and another phase whose `output`
176:   // is `X` collide because dependents naming `X` can't tell which
177:   // producer is meant.
178:   for (const phase of phases) {
179:     const key = outputKey(phase);
180:     if (key === phase.name) continue;
181:     const collidingPhase = phasesByName.get(key);
182:     if (collidingPhase !== undefined && collidingPhase.name !== phase.name) {
183:       throw new PhaseRunnerError(
184:         'duplicate-output',
185:         `Phase runner rejected: phase "${phase.name}" output "${key}" collides with phase name "${collidingPhase.name}"`,
186:         phase.name,
187:       );
188:     }
189:   }
190: 
191:   for (const phase of phases) {
192:     for (const dep of phase.inputs) {
193:       if (!phaseNamesByOutputKey.has(dep) && !phasesByName.has(dep)) {
194:         throw new PhaseRunnerError(
195:           'missing-dependency',
196:           `Phase runner rejected: phase "${phase.name}" depends on unknown phase "${dep}"`,
197:           phase.name,
198:         );
199:       }
200:     }
201:   }
202: 
203:   // Kahn's algorithm — keeps incoming-edge counts and processes
204:   // any node with zero remaining edges. Stable iteration order
205:   // (insertion order of `phases`) makes failure messages
206:   // deterministic.
207:   const incomingEdges = new Map<string, number>();
208:   const dependents = new Map<string, string[]>();
209:   for (const phase of phases) {
210:     incomingEdges.set(phase.name, phase.inputs.length);
211:     for (const dep of phase.inputs) {
212:       const depPhaseName = phaseNamesByOutputKey.get(dep) ?? dep;
213:       const list = dependents.get(depPhaseName) ?? [];
214:       list.push(phase.name);
215:       dependents.set(depPhaseName, list);
216:     }
217:   }
218: 
219:   const ready: string[] = [];
220:   for (const phase of phases) {
221:     if ((incomingEdges.get(phase.name) ?? 0) === 0) {
222:       ready.push(phase.name);
223:     }
224:   }
225: 
226:   const order: string[] = [];
227:   while (ready.length > 0) {
228:     const next = ready.shift()!;
229:     order.push(next);
230:     for (const dependent of dependents.get(next) ?? []) {
231:       const remaining = (incomingEdges.get(dependent) ?? 0) - 1;
232:       incomingEdges.set(dependent, remaining);
233:       if (remaining === 0) {
234:         ready.push(dependent);
235:       }
236:     }
237:   }
238: 
239:   if (order.length !== phases.length) {
240:     const stuck = phases
241:       .filter(p => (incomingEdges.get(p.name) ?? 0) > 0)
242:       .map(p => p.name);
243:     throw new PhaseRunnerError(
244:       'cycle-detected',
245:       `Phase runner rejected: cycle detected among phases [${stuck.join(', ')}]`,
246:       stuck[0],
247:     );
248:   }
249: 
250:   return order;
251: }
252: 
253: /**
254:  * Execute the phases in topological order.
255:  *
256:  * - Each phase receives a `deps` object containing ONLY outputs of
257:  *   phases listed in its `inputs`. Other phase outputs in the run
258:  *   are not visible — this is the dependency-only passing contract.
259:  * - On failure, throws `PhaseRunnerError('phase-failure')` carrying
260:  *   the offending phase name in `phaseName` and the original error as
261:  *   `cause` for failure attribution.
262:  *
263:  * Returns the full output map keyed by each phase's `output` (or
264:  * `name` when `output` is omitted).
265:  */
266: export async function runPhases(phases: readonly Phase[], signal?: AbortSignal): Promise<PhaseOutputs> {
267:   const order = topologicalSort(phases);
268:   const phasesByName = new Map<string, Phase>();
269:   for (const phase of phases) phasesByName.set(phase.name, phase);
270: 
271:   const outputs: PhaseOutputs = {};
272:   const phaseNamesByOutputKey = new Map<string, string>();
273:   for (const phase of phases) phaseNamesByOutputKey.set(outputKey(phase), phase.name);
274: 
275:   for (const phaseName of order) {
276:     // Cooperative cancellation. When the caller's deadline aborts (e.g.
277:     // ensure-ready's indexWithTimeout), stop before the next phase instead of
278:     // running the whole pipeline to completion in the background and discarding
279:     // the result. Checked at phase boundaries to keep it cheap and safe.
280:     if (signal?.aborted) {
281:       throw new Error(`runPhases aborted before phase "${phaseName}"`);
282:     }
283:     const phase = phasesByName.get(phaseName)!;
284:     const deps: Record<string, unknown> = {};
285:     for (const inputKey of phase.inputs) {
286:       // Resolve to the canonical output key so the value is published
287:       // under the same name the dependency declared.
288:       const producerName = phaseNamesByOutputKey.get(inputKey) ?? inputKey;
289:       const producer = phasesByName.get(producerName);
290:       const producerOutputKey = producer ? outputKey(producer) : inputKey;
291:       deps[inputKey] = outputs[producerOutputKey];
292:     }
293: 
294:     try {
295:       const value = await phase.run(deps);
296:       outputs[outputKey(phase)] = value;
297:     } catch (cause) {
298:       const message = cause instanceof Error ? cause.message : String(cause);
299:       throw new PhaseRunnerError(
300:         'phase-failure',
301:         `Phase "${phase.name}" failed: ${message}`,
302:         phase.name,
303:         { cause },
304:       );
305:     }
306:   }
307: 
308:   return outputs;
309: }

496: /** Initialize (or get) the code graph database */
497: export function initDb(dbDir: string): Database.Database {
498:   if (db) return db;
499: 
500:   try {
501:     dbPath = join(dbDir, 'code-graph.sqlite');
502:     db = new Database(dbPath);
503:     // Explicit busy_timeout so concurrent writers wait up to 5s
504:     // for the writer lock instead of throwing SQLITE_BUSY immediately. Set
505:     // BEFORE journal_mode/foreign_keys so any incidental contention during
506:     // PRAGMA setup also benefits from the wait.
